title: Mini Fridge Snack Cabinet
summary: A cabinet sized to house a mini fridge with a drawer above and a shelf that can hold snacks or come out for a taller fridge. Legs, drawer, and a real top.
description:
A small cabinet built around a mini fridge: an open bay that fits a compact fridge, a fixed lower shelf, an optional middle shelf for snacks (leave it out and the bay swallows a taller fridge), a drawer up top, and a finished slab top. It's the piece for a dorm, bonus room, or office nook — hides the fridge and gives you drawer and shelf storage in the same footprint.

The carcass is plywood ripped to 15-3/4" strips (called '1x16' in the plan) on 2x2 legs, trimmed to look built. Go formaldehyde-free on the sheet good if you can — it lives in a room where people eat. The bottom shelf sits 3/4" up from the bottom of the sides and is fixed; the middle shelf can ride on shelf pins so you can adjust or remove it for fridge clearance.

The drawer is the one part that demands precision. Build it to the opening and the specific 16" euro slides, install it in its highest position first, and confirm it slides sweetly before committing. It sits inset 3/4" so the applied face lands flush — attach that face last with an even gap all around.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Cut into 15-3/4" strips (the '1x16' in this plan). Formaldehyde-free preferred.

materials.name: Shelf pins
materials.note: For the optional adjustable middle shelf.

cutList.note: One is the optional middle/adjustable shelf.

steps.title: Trim the sides
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim to the plywood sides with 1-1/4" finish nails and wood glue as shown. This dresses the raw plywood edges.

steps.title: Add the legs
steps.body: Add the 2x2 legs to the sides with the pocket-hole jig as shown in the diagram.

steps.title: Install the shelves
steps.body: Install the shelves. The bottom shelf sits up 3/4" from the bottom of the sides — attach it fixed. The middle shelf can be made adjustable with shelf pins, or left out entirely so the bay holds a taller fridge.

steps.title: Trim the bottom shelf and add the top support
steps.body: Trim the bottom shelf and add the top front/back support as shown in the diagram — these carry the drawer and stiffen the opening.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top, squaring up the edges and making sure it's flush, then attach it to the cabinet. You can also drive 2" screws up from the 2x2s into the top.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Build the drawer, adjusting for square and attaching the plywood bottom. Fasten the drawer members to the drawer bottom.

steps.title: Install the drawer and face
steps.body: Install the slides in the cabinet with the drawer in its highest possible position. Fit the drawer and confirm it slides nicely — it needs to sit inset 3/4" for the face to inset. Once happy, attach the face with an even gap all around using 1-1/4" finish nails and glue. Add the bottom trim last, sand, and finish.

images.alt: Cabinet housing a mini fridge with a drawer above and a finished top
